drivers.title: International organisations
drivers.body: UNICEF, the Council of Europe, and the ITU are described as catalytic actors across countries with advanced frameworks.

drivers.title: Institutional champions
drivers.body: Regulators and agencies in Ghana, Kenya, Cameroon, Zambia, and South Africa show what mandate-backed institutional champions look like.

drivers.title: Coalition pressure
drivers.body: Coalition-based advocacy and technical partnerships are described as more effective than generic appeals to government.

barriers.title: Competing legislative priorities
barriers.body: COP often loses political bandwidth unless it is framed as a child protection, public health, and productivity issue.

barriers.title: Regulatory capture and platform lobbying
barriers.body: Platform accountability provisions are identified as some of the most politically contested parts of digital legislation.

barriers.title: Surveillance and rights concerns
barriers.body: Uganda, Nigeria, and South Sudan are used as warnings that poorly designed online safety law can be weaponised against rights and civic space.
